_format_note: Meta-harness's own hook registry. Both hooks ship OFF per ADR-0003. The plugin's runtime equivalent (Claude Code) should treat enabled:false as a no-op; flip to true in the user's local copy to opt in. AC-5 verification: jq '.hooks[].enabled' must yield only `false` values when this file is shipped.

hooks.name: session-start-healthcheck
hooks.description: On every Claude Code session start in a directory that already has a harness, run /meta-harness:manage --json-only --silent and write the report under .meta-harness/reports/. Opt-in. ADR-0003 default-OFF rationale: silent disk writes from a hook are a surprise vector; the operator must consciously enable.

hooks.name: stop-evaluate
hooks.description: On Claude Code Stop in a harnessed directory, run /meta-harness:evaluate --json-only and write the JSON report under .meta-harness/reports/. Opt-in. Same default-OFF rationale: an evaluate run can take 30-120s and is billable (LLM-as-judge); auto-triggering on every Stop is a footgun. Note: evaluate's stdout redirect is non-atomic (unlike the manage hook which uses --write-report); partial JSON may land on evaluate failure — v0.2 will symmetrize this once evaluate gains --write-report.
